cards.title: A Necessary Ache in Fatherhood
cards.body: Regret is one of fatherhood's greatest teachers, awakening men to their shortcomings. It guides fathers toward repair and helps them become wiser parents.

cards.title: Are You Raising a Boy Who Doesn't Like Sports?
cards.body: Raising non-sporty boys in a culture that prizes athletic talent in males is challenging. Parents must navigate societal expectations around competitiveness and power playing.

cards.title: When the People Who Should Be Proud of You Aren't
cards.body: Few things hurt more than having your accomplishments dismissed by those you love. Psychology offers insight into why family dismissal of achievements happens.

cards.title: What Mattering Changes in the Classroom
cards.body: Schools talk about belonging, but students can belong while feeling invisible. Mattering—not just belonging—is what transforms how children thrive academically.

cards.title: Adult Children on the Front Lines of Caregiving
cards.body: Caring for aging parents is an expected but often stressful phase of adulthood. Balancing a parent's autonomy, safety, and your own well-being requires careful navigation.

cards.title: Family Estrangement: Effects on Wellbeing Are Mixed
cards.body: Estrangement exists on a continuum rather than as a binary condition. Support plays an important role whether or not people seek reconciliation.
